# Feature Specification: Installer Wizard (Initial Setup)

## Status

* **Status:** IN PROGRESS
* **Target Module:** `src/renderer/src/features/wizard-installer/`

---

## 1. Overview
Step-by-step page to septub basic user-data with manual inputs or OAuth with Linkedin, Google, or Github account, with Access Token for Github to fetch profile and projects and file directory save for output documents. Must be simple an easy to manage for the user so can use it immediately after installation.
If Linkedin is used to get basic information, will fill all last inputs at Home view that user need to add manually.
If Github account used, will use account info to fill up the inputs: bio will be used for Professional Title and picture will be used for Avatar.
If Google account used, will use account info to fill up the basic inputs (first name, last name, email).

---

## 2. Specifications
**1. Layout:**
  - Header with step navigator (Step 1, Step 2, Step 3).
    -  Must be w-full, p-5, flex-row justify-between items-center, border-1 border rounded-xl. Will have 3 columns with same space for each child. Consistant gap-2. With p  with border-1 rounded-full with p-10 with text "Step 1" "Step 2" "Step 3". Actual step must have higlight.
    
  - States provided by the layout to the childrens (StepOne, StepTwo, StepThree)
    - firstName: text input (string)
    - lastName: text input (string)
    - email: text input (string)
    - accessTokenGithub: text input (string)
    - outputDirectoryPath: text input (string)
  - Body contains childrens (StepOne, StepTwo, StepThree) that will be displayed based on the current step state.
  - Footer with buttons Back and Next to move in between steps.
    - Button should have py-2 px-5 rounded-xl, hover interactions and active state feedback.
  - Button Back will be hidded in Step 1.
**2. Step 1: Accounts or Manually Inputs:**
  -  One container with 2 rows. First row has one column with LinkedIn, Google and Github buttons.
    - If used buttons, automatically go no the next step. If Github account was used, skip step-2 and go directly to step-3.
  -  Second row will hace 3 inputs in one column.
  - Por container: space-y-3, items-center, justify-center, flex-column. Divisor between first and second row with text "O" in the middle.
**3. Step 2: GitHub Access Token:**
  -  One container with 2 columns. First column must have one input for the GitHub Access Token and  one button to validate the token.
    - If token is valid, continue to step 3 automatically. If NOT valid, show toast notification with human readable text error.
    - Input should be masked to show only dots (••••••••••••••••••••••).
  - Second coulmn, has one column with ol>li of steps to get the access token with first step with anchor to Dev Section in Settings Page of our github account. Must be readable and easy to follow.
**3. Step 3: Target Output Directory Path:**
  -  One container with one column, justify-center and items-center. Must have an input and button in flex-row div for brows the directory where user wants to save documents generated by the app.
  - By default, app will use ~/documents/Currynator/
    - Will create the folder if not exist at the first run. If exist, simply will use it.
---

## 3. Behavioral & UI Rules
    - Basic information will use service 'profile-service.ts' to save information using function `saveProfile()`;
    - Tokens wil be saved by `secure.service.ts` with function `saveSecureToken()`
    - Target output directory path will use `settings.service.ts` to save information using function `saveSettings()`;
    - For OAtuh, use client_id and client_secret provided in the enviroment file .env.
    - OAuth connection have to get data for field profile.
